questions.q: Explain the concept of comparative advantage and its importance in international trade. What are the benefits of comparative advantage to a country? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Comparative advantage is a concept developed by David Ricardo, which states that countries should specialize in producing goods for which they have a lower opportunity cost compared to other countries. This concept is crucial in international trade as it allows countries to maximize their output and efficiency by focusing on the production of goods for which they have a comparative advantage. The benefits of comparative advantage to a country include increased efficiency, higher productivity, and increased exports, leading to economic growth and development.
marking_scheme: Importance of comparative advantage in international trade (1 mark)
Benefits of comparative advantage to a country (3 marks)

questions.q: Describe the role of India in international trade. What are the main advantages and challenges faced by India in the global market? (5 marks)
questions.answer: India plays a significant role in international trade, with a growing economy and increasing participation in the global market. The main advantages of India in the global market include a large and growing consumer base, a skilled and competitive workforce, and a favorable business environment. The challenges faced by India in the global market include a large trade deficit, a complex regulatory environment, and high transportation costs.
marking_scheme: Role of India in international trade (1 mark)
Advantages of India in the global market (2 marks)
Challenges faced by India in the global market (2 marks)
questions.topic: Role of India in International Trade

questions.q: What are the main types of international trade agreements that India has signed? Explain the benefits and drawbacks of these agreements. (5 marks)
questions.answer: India has signed several types of international trade agreements, including the World Trade Organization (WTO) agreements, the South Asian Free Trade Area (SAFTA) agreement, and the Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP) agreement. The benefits of these agreements include increased access to foreign markets, reduced tariffs, and increased economic growth. The drawbacks of these agreements include potential job losses, increased competition, and a loss of sovereignty.
marking_scheme: Types of international trade agreements signed by India (1 mark)

questions.q: A country imports 1000 units of a product from another country at a price of $10 per unit. Calculate the total value of the import in dollars. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Total Value of Import = Number of Units × Price per Unit
Total Value of Import = 1000 × 10
Total Value of Import = $10,000
marking_scheme: Calculation of total value of import (5 marks)

questions.q: A country exports 500 units of a product to another country at a price of $15 per unit. Calculate the total value of the export in dollars. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Total Value of Export = Number of Units × Price per Unit
Total Value of Export = 500 × 15
Total Value of Export = $7,500
marking_scheme: Calculation of total value of export (5 marks)

questions.q: A country imports 2000 units of a product from another country at a price of $20 per unit. If the country also exports 1000 units of the same product at a price of $12 per unit, calculate the trade balance in dollars. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Total Value of Import = Number of Units × Price per Unit
Total Value of Import = 2000 × 20
Total Value of Import = $40,000
Total Value of Export = Number of Units × Price per Unit
Total Value of Export = 1000 × 12
Total Value of Export = $12,000
Trade Balance = Total Value of Import - Total Value of Export
Trade Balance = $40,000 - $12,000
Trade Balance = $28,000
marking_scheme: Calculation of total value of import (3 marks)
Calculation of total value of export (2 marks)
